_article: XXXIX.7 — Travel Graph is a first-class compiled artifact. User directive: 'NPCs don't pathfind over blocks. They pathfind over civilization.'
_description: Schema for the COMPILED Travel Graph — a navigation graph where nodes are waypoints (settlements, crossroads, bridges, passes, ferries) and edges are RoadSegments with semantic properties (length, terrain crossed, danger, toll, seasonality). Caravans, patrols, refugees, wandering cultivators, and NPCs all pathfind over THIS graph, not over Minecraft blocks. The Minecraft adapter renders the road visually; the simulation moves entities along these edges.

_design_notes.why_separate_from_relationship_graph: The travel graph is queried at high frequency (every tick for every mobile entity) and needs O(1) edge-weight and neighbor lookups. It is a projection of the relationship_graph's connected_to/traverses/leads_to edges into a weighted directed graph optimized for pathfinding.
pathfinding_consumers: Refugee routing (when a settlement is threatened)
Player fast-travel (if/when added — must respect canon travel times)

waypoints.waypoint_id: string — stable; may equal a settlement_id, bridge_id, pass_id, or a pure crossroads id
waypoints.entity_type: enum — settlement | crossroads | bridge | mountain_pass | ferry | shrine_waypoint | sect_gate
waypoints.facilities: array — [rest, trade, shrine, sect_access, ferry, heal] — what a traveler can do here
waypoints.compiled_state_ref: string — reference to the waypoint's full state (if it is a settlement, its settlement_graph entry)

edges.edge_id: string — stable; corresponds to a RoadSegment entity id in the blueprint
edges.road_id: string — the Road entity this segment belongs to
edges.length_blocks: int — canonical block distance (drives travel time)

travel_cost.chokepoint_required: string|null — bridge_id/pass_id/ferry_id that must be traversed (and may be impassable)
travel_cost.seasonality: object — {season: modifier} — winter mountain pass may be 0.0 (impassable)
travel_cost.toll: object|null — {amount, currency, toll_keeper_id} — a cost beyond time

edges.danger_class: enum — safe | low | moderate | high | deadly — driven by bandit_momentum and beast_momentum on this segment
edges.current_threat_exposure: float 0.0–1.0 — live, updated by bandit/beast momentum; drives caravan_momentum ambush threshold
edges.maintenance_state: enum — excellent | good | fair | poor | ruined — affects speed and danger (a ruined road is slow and bandit-prone)

routes.canonical_travel_time_ticks: int — for canon distance fidelity (user directive: 'Heng Yue is three days away' should literally be true)
routes.primary_users: array — [caravan_id, patrol_id, ...] — trajectories currently using this route

indices.by_danger: sorted by danger_class — for 'where is it safe to travel' queries

queries_supported: path(waypoint_a, waypoint_b, constraints?) -> [edge_id] — shortest/safest path respecting constraints (season, toll budget, danger tolerance)
route_position(entity_id) -> {edge_id, position_along_edge 0-1} — where is this mobile entity right now
eta(waypoint_a, waypoint_b, speed_class) -> int ticks — canon travel time

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — Road, RoadSegment, Bridge, MountainPass, Ferry entities + connected_to/traverses/leads_to relationships
_runtime_consumers: ChunkRenderer (renders the road visually along the edge geometry)
